name: HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (FY 236)
events: 21 Dec 1941 In a signal timed 1000 A/21, the Capt. S.5 sent the following orders to HMS H 32 (Lt. J.S. Stevens, DSC, RN) and HMS P 38 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN); Leave patrol after dark tonight routed as follows; A) H 32 on the surface at the Commanding Officers discretion through for Falmouth. Keep east of 04°15'W, until daylight. B) P 38 through position 50°00'N, 01°45'W to rendezvous in position 50°31'N, 00°41'W at 0845 A/22 for Portsmouth. C) Bombing restrictions has been arranged.

In a follow up signal timed 1749 A/21, the Capt. S.5 signalled the following to HMS P 38; 1) HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (T/Skr. L. Ford, RNR) and HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. W.M. Smith, RNR) will be at rendezvous at 0845 A/22. 2) Sanctuary in force from 0745 A/22 to 1200 A/22. 3) Keep south of 50°25'N, until 0730 A/22.

In a follow up signal timed 1751 A/21, the Capt. S.5 signalled the following to HMS H 32; 1) HMS Loch Park (T/Skr. R.H. Maulkinson, RNR) will be at the rendezvous at 1200 A/22. 2) Convoy PW 84 is expected at Falmouth at 1100 A/22. No other movements affect you. (1)
22 Dec 1941 Around 1240 A/22, HMS P 38 (Lt. R.J. Hemingway, DSC, RN) ended her 3rd war patrol at Gosport (Portsmouth).
She was escorted in by HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (T/Skr. L. Ford, RNR) and HMS Kingston Topaz (Skr. W.M. Smith, RNR) which had joined around 0850 A/22. (2)

15 Jul 1942 HMS H 43 (Lt. A.R. Profit, DSC, RN) arrived at Plymouth where she conducted A/S exercises with HMS La Nantaise (Skr. S.J. Cory, RNR), HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (A/Skr.Lt. M.A. Smith, RD, RNR), ORP Slazak (Lt.Cdr. R. Nalecz-Tyminski, ORP), HMS Ambrose Pare (Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR), HMS Olvina (Skr. J. Howson, RNR), HMS Cambridgeshire (T/Lt. H.F.B. Ashby, RNVR) and HMS Grimsby Town (Skr. W.A. Cormack, RNR). (3)

25 Feb 1943 HMS P 511 (Lt. C.R. Pelly, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Grimsby Town (Skr. W.A. Cormack, RNR), HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (Skr.Lt. M.A. Smith, RD, RNR) and HMS Ambrose Pare (Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR). (4)
2 May 1944 HMS Seraph (Lt. T. Russell-Walling, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with HMS Lord Stanhope (A/Skr.Lt. S.G. Jinks, RNR) and HMS Kingston Chrysoberyl (A/Skr.Lt. H. Rich, RNR). (5)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
